task: Reason about Clive's next architectural step

converged_candidate.title: Make ScreenClassifier command-aware in one PR, gated on a 30% escalation-rate drop on the existing scenario bench
converged_candidate.thesis: Thread last_command through ScreenClassifier.classify() and add an INFORMATIONAL_NONZERO allowlist (git diff --quiet, grep, test, [, cmp -s, diff -q, pgrep). Validate via existing evals/observation/scenarios.py + metrics.py:RunResult.cost_tokens. Merge iff median shell-mode cost_tokens drops >=30% AND missed_rate does not increase versus current phase1 row.
first_pr_files: tests/test_observation.py (6 cases incl. default-None backward compat)
converged_candidate.success_metric: On post-PR bench: median cost_tokens for shell-mode -30% vs phase1 row AND missed_rate <= phase1 missed_rate. Both numbers already produced by metrics.aggregate().

critique_themes: Observation-loop escalation precision is the load-bearing uncalibrated coefficient under Clive's cost stack
Persistent screen capture has unresolved privacy concerns (kubectl logs, env, printenv leak secrets)
Strategy-pattern abstractions are premature before a second concrete strategy exists
Phase 2 speculation (CLIVE_SPECULATE=1) cannot default-on without an escalation-precision number
Existing eval harness (evals/observation/, metrics.py:RunResult.cost_tokens) is underutilized — the corpus shape exists; only the experiment is missing

quality_signals.judge_split_axis: perf/dist-sys/pragmatic (A) vs. tech-lead/long-lived (AB) — informative disposition divergence, not noise

disposition_note: Round 3's final winner is A, not the synthesis. This is a meaningful signal: it indicates the synthesizer's instinct to add scaffolding (decision log + replay tool + strategy seam) was rejected by 3 of 5 judges in favor of a falsifiable single-PR experiment. The runner-up AB remains a viable round-4 candidate IF the round-3 experiment lands and a second strategy is needed.
